title: Traveling with Kyle - Peru Part 7/7, Bolivia Part 1

transcript.text: Okay. Yeah, so I'm hiking up this mountain. I'm hiking about an hour up. I would have gotten to the top of the 6,000 meter mountain. It would have been a beautiful view. I would have had fun, but I'm hiking and I finally, see, I'm not thinking clearly. I'm still like on the weed, you know, month of being high off my ass, not even being sober.

I was just like, you know what? I am not really in the mood for this. You know, I don't want to ride mountains anymore. So I turned around and I went back to my bike and I rode all the way down the hill, down the side of this hill, really steep road, which I had actually already ridden up all the way past where I was over these switchbacks on the side of the mountain, really high, maybe, I don't know, 13,000 feet or something.

And then down again to this military installation. So I'd already ridden that, but I rode down it again really fast. And when the first time I did it, it was weird because I rode past some like slaughterhouse or something.

It was like this big, big barn where they were like, it was four in the morning. No, the sun hadn't come yet. I heard like thousands of animals just screaming. It was like, I was in hell. You imagine hell every night, just screaming at the top of their lungs. I'm like, what are these animals screaming for?

They must have been packed in there. They're just miserable. I don't know. It was the creepiest feeling I've ever seen in my entire life. But anyways, I ride down and then I ride back to my hotel room and I'm like, I guess I'm not into Miami mountains anymore.
